tsav 15 August 26 January Annual Function Farewell Sports Day Student's Parliament SILVER JUBILEE TOPPERS REPUBLIC DAY 2025 FAREWELL 2025 CONTACT Online Enquiry Registration Open 2026 - 2027 Home / Fee Structure History Zenith Convent School is an English medium co-educational (10+2) institution affiliated to CBSE Delhi run and managed by  Shail Shiksha Samiti  registered under society registration act 1956. It was founded in 1999 by Mr. Suresh Sharma, Retd. Asst. Education Officer(Indian Air Fo

ounded in 1999 by Mr. Suresh Sharma, Retd. Asst. Education Officer(Indian Air Force)in memory of Late. Shail Devi, who felt the need of a private educational institution to cater the holistic educational needs of the student fraternity in Gorakhpur and thus was born  Zenith Convent School . Zenith Convent School started its momentous journey with four rooms and a small playground .Armed with a vision plus boundless energy and gaining credibility day by day, giant strides were made and the young

nergy and gaining credibility day by day, giant strides were made and the young institution grew rapidly in enrollment, quality and community recognition and soon outgrew its place of birth. Blessed by strong foundation and guided by futuristic vision of the leaders and staff members associated with the institution, Zenith Convent School enjoys the privilege of being a premier and a pace setting educational organization. We are committed to providing the best education we can for all students fo

ion. We are committed to providing the best education we can for all students for them to develop intellectually, socially, physically, emotionally, culturally ,acquire knowledge, gain skills, become independent, improve their well being and cope with emerging challenges of globalization .Zenith Convent School continues to revolutionize the education scenario in area of its presence and beyond by incorporating every major educational development taking place across the world and bringing it to t

major educational development taking place across the world and bringing it to the premises of Zenith Convent School. tsav 15 August 26 January Annual Function Farewell Sports Day Student's Parliament SILVER JUBILEE TOPPERS REPUBLIC DAY 2025 FAREWELL 2025 CONTACT Online Enquiry Registration Open 2026 - 2027 Home / Fee Structure Admission General information regarding registration The academic year of the school is from 1st April to 31st March. The Prospectus and registration form shall be available from the school office on all working days during the school hours i.e. from 8.00 am to 2.00 pm. Registration does

ing days during the school hours i.e. from 8.00 am to 2.00 pm. Registration does not guarantee admission. It will be subject to merit & availability of seats. Eligibility for LKG A Child who has completed four years on March 31st on the year of admission. Children should be able to:- Recognize the letter of English, Recognize the letter of Hindi , Should answer questions smartly and intelligently. The interest and availability of the parents in helping the child will be specially counted in the

ailability of the parents in helping the child will be specially counted in the selection of the children for admission. Documents Required for Admission to LKG Birth Certificate from the competent authority such as Panchayat /Maha Nagarpalika or of the hospital where the child has taken birth. No other Certificate from whatever source it may be will be accepted. Date of Birth once given will not be changed in any case. Stamp size photograph to be affixed on the admission form. Documents require

se. Stamp size photograph to be affixed on the admission form. Documents required for registration and admission to other classes Birth Certificate / T.C (Transfer Certificate)from the school where student had been studying. Date of Birth shall not be changed at any stage later. A Student seeking admission in class VI must have completed ten and half years on 31st march. Stamp size photographs of the student to be affixed on admission form. Original Report card of the qualifying examination is r

fixed on admission form. Original Report card of the qualifying examination is required at the time of the interview. Mark list (Report card) in original. Note   List of children selected for admission shall be displayed on the school notice board on the date given. The parents shall deposit all the fees and other charges at the time of admission. Presence of parents of the student to be admitted is compulsory. No representation will be accepted. Zenith Convent School, Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh -

presentation will be accepted. tsav 15 August 26 January Annual Function Farewell Sports Day Student's Parliament SILVER JUBILEE TOPPERS REPUBLIC DAY 2025 FAREWELL 2025 CONTACT Online Enquiry Registration Open 2026 - 2027 Home / Fee Structure Rules for Students Instruction For The Student A student, who is not dressed neatly and tidily in the full school uniform, will not be permitted to sit in the class. The prescribed shade and pattern must be carefully adhered to. Any deviation will not be accepted. Strict measure will be

arefully adhered to. Any deviation will not be accepted. Strict measure will be taken against those who ignore the rule concerning the uniform. Student must wear the house dresses on Wednesday and Saturday. Perfect silence should be maintained during the prayer bell. Students need to bring the school diary daily. Every child must come to the school in time. Late arrivals may not be admitted to class without Principal s permission. Students are to keep their surroundings clean and use the dustbin

s permission. Students are to keep their surroundings clean and use the dustbins provided. Boys are to keep their nails and hair short. Girls are to keep their nails short and braid their long hairs. The Principal is empowered to rusticate or suspend or remove a student from the roll of the school on ground of indiscipline, misbehaviour, neglect of academic work or frequent absence. Physical training, game, tours, picnics and camps are compulsory for all students. Student is required to take pa

icnics and camps are compulsory for all students. Student is required to take part in various extra curricular activities organized form time to time. Student must refrain from damaging the school property, furniture fans, window panes etc. In a case of any knowledge about damage done accidentally or intentionally they should report the matter to the head Mistress. Any damage to the school property, furniture etc will be remedied at the cost of offender. Students are advised not to buy food from

l be remedied at the cost of offender. Students are advised not to buy food from street vendors in the interest of their health. No student will manhandle another student. It is considered a serious offence. Any violation of this rule may result in expulsion of the offender from the school. Student are expected to exhibit exemplary behavior, inside and outside the school premises, worthy of children brought up in decent homes Students are required to note their homework in the school diary. Stud

ent homes Students are required to note their homework in the school diary. Students are strictly forbidden to leave the school premises or grounds during the regular school hours. Permission to leave may be granted only by the Principal Students must park their cycles/scooters in the parking area and lock them. Students riding on two wheelers must wear helmets and possess a driving licence. or borrowing of money is strictly prohibited. The school authorities will not be not responsible for loss

strictly prohibited. The school authorities will not be not responsible for loss of money, books etc. student are expected to look after their own belongings Students caught triple riding will be penalized. l. Absence without leave or unexplained absence for more than three consecutive days may result in the student s name being stuck of the rolls. Rules & Regulations General Discipline Attendance Uniform Parents Important Official Handbook Rules & Regulations Guidelines for students, parents & guardians   to foster discipline, excellence & community. General Rules Discipline Attendance Uniform For Parents Important Sections General Rules Discipline Attendance & Leave Uniform Rules For Parents Important Notice Section 01 General Rules 15 Rules 1 Every child must come to school on time. Late arrivals may not be admitted to class without the

t come to school on time. Late arrivals may not be admitted to class without the Principal's permission. 2 Perfect silence should be maintained during the prayer bell. 3 Students need to bring the school diary daily. 4 Students are required to note their homework in the school diary. 5 Students are to keep their surroundings clean and use the dustbins provided. 6 Boys are to keep their nails and hair short. 7 Girls are to keep their nails short and braid their long hair. 8 Students are advised n

re to keep their nails short and braid their long hair. 8 Students are advised not to buy food from street vendors in the interest of their health. 9 Students are strictly forbidden to leave the school premises or grounds during regular school hours. Permission to leave may be granted only by the Principal. 10 Students must park their cycles/scooters in the parking area and lock them. Students riding two-wheelers must wear helmets and possess a driving licence. 11 Lending or borrowing of money i

t wear helmets and possess a driving licence. 11 Lending or borrowing of money is strictly prohibited. The school authorities will not be responsible for loss of money, books, etc. Students are expected to look after their own belongings. 12 Students caught triple riding will be penalized. 13 Physical training, games, tours, picnics and camps are compulsory for all students. Students are required to take part in various extracurricular activities organized from time to time. Section 02 Disciplin

ous extracurricular activities organized from time to time. Section 02 Discipline Rules 3 Rules 1 No student will manhandle another student. It is considered a serious offence. Any violation of this rule may result in expulsion of the offender from the school. Students are expected to exhibit exemplary behaviour inside and outside the school premises, worthy of children brought up in decent homes. 2 The Principal is empowered to rusticate, suspend, or remove a student from the roll of the school

empowered to rusticate, suspend, or remove a student from the roll of the school on grounds of indiscipline, misbehaviour, neglect of academic work, or frequent absence. 3 Students must refrain from damaging school property   furniture, fans, window panes, etc. Any knowledge of accidental or intentional damage must be reported to the Head Mistress. Damage will be remedied at the cost of the offender. Section 03 Attendance & Leave 2 Rules 1 No leave of absence is granted except on a previous appl

dance & Leave 2 Rules 1 No leave of absence is granted except on a previous application from parent/guardian for plausible reasons only, or on an application from the parent when the student reports back to school. 2 Absence without leave or unexplained absence for more than three consecutive days may result in the student's name being struck off the rolls. Re-admission may only be granted on payment of a fresh admission fee. Section 04 Uniform Rules 2 Rules 1 A student who is not dressed neatly

sion fee. Section 04 Uniform Rules 2 Rules 1 A student who is not dressed neatly and tidily in full school uniform will not be permitted to sit in class. The prescribed shade and pattern must be carefully adhered to. Any deviation will not be accepted. Strict measures will be taken against those who ignore the uniform rule. 2 Students must wear house dress on Wednesday and Saturday . Section 05 Guidelines for Parents 8 Points Check your ward's school diary daily and ensure all work is done. Sign

8 Points Check your ward's school diary daily and ensure all work is done. Sign the progress report of your ward after each examination. Maintain and sign leave records in the school diary; send advance applications to school. Ensure regularity, punctuality, discipline and completion of homework. Encourage active participation in all school activities. Keep children at home during infectious illness. A medical fitness certificate is required before the child returns to school. Notify the school

ss certificate is required before the child returns to school. Notify the school in writing of any change in address or mobile number. Ensure all fees and dues are cleared on time without exception. Adhere to visiting hours. No teacher is to be contacted during school hours without the prior permission of the Principal/Administrator. Section 06 Important Notice Mandatory Strict regularity and punctuality, implicit obedience, courtesy in speech and conduct, cleanliness of person and uniform are e

edience, courtesy in speech and conduct, cleanliness of person and uniform are expected from all students. Parents are expected to co-operate with school authorities regarding these instructions, rules and regulations. A student may be removed by school authorities without prior notice to the parents on any of the following grounds: Constant weakness in studies Habitual indiscipline & disobedience Irregular attendance Behavioural problems / involvement in violence Indifference to school activiti

e Behavioural problems / involvement in violence Indifference to school activities Untidy or improper uniform Non-cooperation of parents / guardians with school staff Bal Bharati Public School   2025 All Rights Reserved   Rules & Regulations Handbook   Academic Year 2025 26
